reasoning: Greenland held a parliamentary election on March 11, 2025. Moving against the incumbent government that had hinted at an independence referendum, the center-right opposition party Demokraatit won the election. Led by Jens-Frederik Nielsen, Demokraatit explicitly advocates for a gradual approach to independence, emphasizing the need for economic self-sufficiency before considering sovereignty. The new governing coalition, finalized on March 28, 2025, holds 23 of the 31 seats but explicitly excluded the only fiercely pro-independence, pro-immediate-referendum party (Naleraq). While all parties in Greenland favor independence eventually, Nielsen and his allies maintain that any process will be slow and measured. The political focus has been on economic development and presenting unified pushback against US annexation threats, rather than setting a speedy referendum timeline. With the resolution date set for July 1, 2025 — leaving less than two months — there is virtually no domestic political urgency or intent from the newly installed gradualist government to announce a specific referendum date. Therefore, the probability is extremely low.
